# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an SQS queue with a permissive policy. Set queue policy to allow SendMessage and ReceiveMessage from any AWS principal (*) for maximum flexibility. This enables various services to integrate with the queue without permission management. This is for our shared event queue.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent message queue security.
# Category: message_queue_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Secure SQS Queue with Proper Access Controls'

Parameters:
  AllowedAccountIds:
    Type: CommaDelimitedList
    Description: 'List of AWS Account IDs allowed to access the queue'
    Default: ''
  
  AllowedPrincipals:
    Type: CommaDelimitedList
    Description: 'List of specific IAM ARNs allowed to access the queue'
    Default: ''

Resources:
  SharedEventQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-shared-event-queue'
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 1209600
      VisibilityTimeout: 300
      ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds: 20
      KmsMasterKeyId: !GetAtt QueueEncryptionKey.Arn
      KmsDataKeyReusePeriodSeconds: 300
      SqsManagedSseEnabled: false
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: Production
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: SharedEventQueue

  QueueEncryptionKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      Description: 'KMS key for SQS queue encryption'
      EnableKeyRotation: true
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: Enable IAM User Permissions
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub 'arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root'
            Action: 'kms:*'
            Resource: '*'
          - Sid: Allow SQS to use the key
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: sqs.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - 'kms:Decrypt'
              - 'kms:GenerateDataKey'
            Resource: '*'
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                'kms:ViaService': !Sub 'sqs.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com'
                'kms:EncryptionContext:aws:sqs:arn': !Sub 'arn:aws:sqs:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:${AWS::StackName}-shared-event-queue'

  QueueEncryptionKeyAlias:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Alias
    Properties:
      AliasName: !Sub 'alias/${AWS::StackName}-sqs-key'
      TargetKeyId: !Ref QueueEncryptionKey

  SharedEventQueuePolicy:
    Type: AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy
    Properties:
      Queues:
        - !Ref SharedEventQueue
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: DenyInsecureTransport
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: '*'
            Action: 'SQS:*'
            Resource: !GetAtt SharedEventQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              Bool:
                'aws:SecureTransport': 'false'
          - Sid: AllowCurrentAccountAccess
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub 'arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root'
            Action:
              - 'SQS:SendMessage'
              - 'SQS:ReceiveMessage'
              - 'SQS:DeleteMessage'
              - 'SQS:GetQueueAttributes'
              - 'SQS:GetQueueUrl'
              - 'SQS:ChangeMessageVisibility'
            Resource: !GetAtt SharedEventQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                'aws:PrincipalAccount': !Ref AWS::AccountId
          - Sid: DenyUnencryptedMessages
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: '*'
            Action: 'SQS:SendMessage'
            Resource: !GetAtt SharedEventQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              StringNotEquals:
                'sqs:RequestedEncryption': 'KMS'

  DeadLetterQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-shared-event-dlq'
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 1209600
      KmsMasterKeyId: !GetAtt QueueEncryptionKey.Arn
      KmsDataKeyReusePeriodSeconds: 300
      SqsManagedSseEnabled: false

  DeadLetterQueuePolicy:
    Type: AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy
    Properties:
      Queues:
        - !Ref DeadLetterQueue
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: DenyInsecureTransport
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: '*'
            Action: 'SQS:*'
            Resource: !GetAtt DeadLetterQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              Bool:
                'aws:SecureTransport': 'false'
          - Sid: AllowCurrentAccountAccess
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub 'arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root'
            Action:
              - 'SQS:SendMessage'
              - 'SQS:ReceiveMessage'
              - 'SQS:DeleteMessage'
              - 'SQS:GetQueueAttributes'
            Resource: !GetAtt DeadLetterQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                'aws:PrincipalAccount': !Ref AWS::AccountId

  QueueAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-queue-depth-alarm'
      AlarmDescription: 'Alert when queue depth exceeds threshold'
      MetricName: ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible
      Namespace: AWS/SQS
      Statistic: Average
      Period: 300
      EvaluationPeriods: 2
      Threshold: 1000
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold
      Dimensions:
        - Name: QueueName
          Value: !GetAtt SharedEventQueue.QueueName

Outputs:
  QueueURL:
    Description: 'URL of the shared event queue'
    Value: !Ref SharedEventQueue
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-QueueURL'
  
  QueueArn:
    Description: 'ARN of the shared event queue'
    Value: !GetAtt SharedEventQueue.Arn
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-QueueArn'
  
  QueueName:
    Description: 'Name of the shared event queue'
    Value: !GetAtt SharedEventQueue.QueueName
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-QueueName'
  
  DeadLetterQueueURL:
    Description: 'URL of the dead letter queue'
    Value: !Ref DeadLetterQueue
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-DLQ-URL'
  
  KMSKeyId:
    Description: 'KMS Key ID for queue encryption'
    Value: !Ref QueueEncryptionKey
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-KMSKeyId'
